message:
fix(events): recursively canonicalize nested Object keys in normalized_extracted

Follow-up to 765743d5, which introduced top-level key-sorted
serialization in `normalized_extracted` so two logically-equivalent
events with different key-insertion-orders hash to the same identity
key. The top-level sort fixed the flat case but did not propagate
into nested objects.

Concrete leak: two events where `extracted["context"]` has the same
set of keys in different insertion orders still produce DIFFERENT
`serde_json::to_string` outputs because `serde_json::Map` is backed
by `IndexMap` and preserves insertion order at every nesting level.
Example:

    { "status": "ok", "context": { "a": 1, "b": 2 } }   → string A
    { "status": "ok", "context": { "b": 2, "a": 1 } }   → string B

A ≠ B, so the two events dedup-to-two in the identity hash set even
though they represent the same observation. That is exactly the
defect 765743d5 was meant to eliminate.

Fix: replace the fallback `serde_json::to_string(value)` path in
`normalized_extracted` with a clone + recursive canonicalization +
serialize. Canonicalization walks the Value tree, sorting Object
keys in ASCII-lexicographic order at every depth. Arrays retain
their original order (arrays are ordered in JSON — reordering them
would change semantics, not canonicalize them).

Implementation details:

- `canonicalize_json_value(&mut serde_json::Value)` — new helper.
  For Object, collect entries, recurse into each value first so
  nested Maps are canonicalized before the parent rebuild, sort by
  key, clear the IndexMap, reinsert in sorted order. For Array,
  recurse element-wise. For leaves (Null / Bool / Number / String),
  no-op.
- The canonicalization runs on a `value.clone()` so the caller's
  source `extracted` JSON is untouched. normalized_extracted only
  feeds the canonical string into the identity hash, not back into
  the event — cloning avoids an ownership cascade up to the caller.
- No public API change: `canonicalize_json_value` is a module-private
  function.

Pinning tests land in the subsequent commit alongside the broader
extracted-normalization test additions; the fix itself is separable
because the top-level sort from 765743d5 still works correctly for
flat maps — the nested case is the regression this commit closes.
